After watching Volume 1, especially the last episode, the only theory I can come up with is that Vecna willingly gave powers to Will Byers and wants all of them to work together to stop what's happening in the Upside Down. He is himself a target of another villain (probably the dragon who will draw), and he's controlling Vecna to revive him. Vecna didn't take Will Byers with him at the last moment; in fact, he said Will is the first who managed to escape this place easily, and at the end, he's going to help him one last time. So, I deduce that Vecna is also stuck in the Upside Down and is using Will and others to destroy the real villain who's emerging. The cast members also confessed something similar on a podcast, saying Vecna is the most misconceived villain.

If this theory is correct, Netflix, hire me! I can work as an actress, a lawyer (I'm qualified), or even a story writer. ✨😂

Let me save you from wasting time

Episode 1 – Everyone’s just vibing, pretending life in Hawkins is totally normal.
Episode 2 – Uh oh, something’s weird again. Who could’ve guessed?
Episode 3 – The main group splits into three smaller groups because... of course they do. Each one’s trying to figure out what’s wrong.
Episode 4 – Time for some random flashbacks and unnecessary time travel energy.
Episode 5 – Cue the awkward teen romance nobody asked for (Will confesses to Mike, because drama).
Episode 6 – All the scattered groups somehow find different clues that magically connect later.
Episode 7 – Minor fights and “epic” action sequences begin.
Episode 8 – Mystery solved in the most ridiculous way possible. Everyone finally reunites for the big boss fight.
Episode 9 – Monster dies, everyone smiles, cue the synth outro.

I'll be completely honest, I am thoroughly uninterested. I hate Vecna as the overall villain of the show because its just so boring. I dont hate Vecna as a villain himself, but I hate him as the villain of the whole show, the one behind everything. I feel like the Mind Flayer was so much more interesting because up until season 4, it was implied that the Mind Flayer was the one behind everything. Now, the big bad is just some guy with parental issues who mind controlled a sentiant cloud. Thats it, thats all there is to the Vecna. The Mind Flayer was mysterious, his intentions were unknown, he was this Lovecraftian monster and that was so much more interesting then, once again, some guy. What I really want from Season 5 is to have the Vecna think he is in control when in reality, the Mind Flayer is in control and he just doesn't know it. Dustin in season four put it perfectly, The Mind Flayer is the president and the Vecna is his 5 star general.
